headline: Wall Street Mounts Pushback on Trillion-Dollar Stablecoin Boom
summary: (Bloomberg) -- For years, banks largely watched stablecoins evolve from a niche cryptocurrency product into a payments network moving tens of trillions of dollars annually. Now they’re dusting off the same collaborative playbook that produced Zelle, betting shared infrastructure is the best way to stop digital dollars from encroaching further on their business.Most Read from BloombergLindsey Graham, Senate Hawk Turned Trump Ally, Dies at 71Hormuz Route Open Despite Iran Declaration, Maritime Gro

headline: Wells Fargo (WFC) Joins Big Banks To Build A Tokenized Deposit Network
summary: Wells Fargo and several major US banks have announced a shared network for tokenized bank deposits operated by The Clearing House. The initiative is intended to create interoperable digital cash inside the banking system that can be used across institutions. The network is positioned as a direct response to the growth of stablecoins and aims to support next generation payments and settlements. For investors following Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC), this move adds a new digital payments angle...

headline: US bank earnings could surprise to the upside as Bank of America highlights strong operating backdrop
summary: US banks could deliver broad earnings beats as strong capital markets activity, resilient economic conditions and improving wealth management flows support second-half 2026 and fiscal 2027 earnings revisions, Bank of America analysts wrote in a note ahead of the sector’s upcoming earnings...
